text: Papa — the split pea soup. Is it 2 cups of dry peas per liter or 1.5? Scaling for 40 at Thursday's catering and my notes say both things somehow 😤
1.5. Always 1.5. Two cups and you get paste, not soup. Your grand-mère would cry. You're using the smoked ham hock, not the sausage?
Ham hock, obviously. I'm not a monster. Jess is helping me with mise en place tomorrow morning so I'm skipping our coffee — sorry, making it up Friday?
Jess is good people. Friday works. Sienna coming to help or is she smart enough to stay away from your prep chaos?
Elle est smarter than both of us. She's doing the whole dessert table so I don't have to think about it. Mon dieu I love her.
Ha. Keep her. See you Friday, mon gars. Don't forget the bay leaf this time.
papa called me this morning saying you're doing a big catering thing thursday. told me you're making the duck confit?? bro save me some
he called YOU about my mise en place 😂 told him over coffee I was doing confit for a corporate lunch at the Hawthorne and now it's a whole family announcement
lol he's so proud of you just let it happen. can i actually come by the hotel thursday or is that weird
closed event Émile, I can't have you wandering the banquet side. Jess would actually end both our careers and she'd be right to
ok fine. but leftover duck > nothing and you KNOW that. I'm just saying
on dit ce qu'on dit. no promises — if there's extra I'll text you before I drive back to Beaverton
Nat, does Grand-père Henri still have that tarte au sucre recipe on paper or did it only ever live in his head? Got a catering client Friday asking for 'something Québécois' and I'm not winging it.
Lol why are you asking ME, call him?? He loves when you call. Also very brave — Beaverton suburbanites discovering sugar pie 😂
Tried, line was busy 20 min straight. Don't start — these clients are paying well, I need it *right*. Not 'Yves improvised' right, actually right.
Ok ok I'll text Maman's side and see if anyone screenshotted it. Give me an hour. Also is Sienna helping or is this a solo catering panic?
Solo, she's got a shift. Jess offered but his knife work on a Friday prep would stress me out. Merci Nat, vraiment.
You're so dramatic. I'll find it. And don't you DARE substitute anything because it's 'easier to source in Oregon.'
